explanation: The phrase "shall prop_0 when prop_1" means that if 'prop_1' is true at any point, then 'prop_0' must also be true at that same point. This is a global implication, meaning it must hold true at all times. Therefore, it translates to G (prop_1 -> prop_0).
The phrase 'A when B' implies that B being true necessitates A being true. This translates to a global implication: whenever 'prop_1' is true, 'prop_0' must also be true.
The phrase 'A when B' implies that A must be true whenever B is true. This translates to a global implication: G(B -> A). Therefore, 'prop_0 when prop_1' becomes G(prop_1 -> prop_0).
The phrase "shall prop_0 when prop_1" means that at any point in time, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must also be true. This is expressed using a global implication.

The phrase 'prop_0 when prop_1' implies that 'prop_0' is true only when 'prop_1' is true. This establishes an implication relationship where 'prop_1' being true necessitates 'prop_0' being true. Since no temporal constraints like 'eventually' or 'next' are mentioned, and the 'when' suggests a general condition, this relationship is considered to hold globally. Therefore, it translates to 'Globally (prop_1 implies prop_0)'. The '(standby)' part provides context but does not alter the logical structure of the LTL formula.

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1 (standby)" means that prop_0 must be true at any moment prop_1 is true. This translates to a global implication, ensuring that if prop_1 holds at any point in time, prop_0 must also hold at that same point.

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when the prop_1 (standby)" implies that if prop_1 is true at any point, then prop_0 must also be true at that same point. The 'shall' indicates that this condition must hold globally, meaning at all times. This translates to a global implication: G (prop_1 -> prop_0).

The statement "prop_0 when prop_1" means that whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also be true at that exact moment. This is a simple implication that must hold at all times, hence the use of the 'G' (Globally) operator.
The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" means that whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also be true at that same moment. This translates to a global implication: G(prop_1 -> prop_0).

The phrase "prop_0 when prop_1" indicates that whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also be true. This is a universal truth over time, meaning it must hold at all moments. Therefore, it translates to a global implication: G(prop_1 -> prop_0). The word "(standby)" clarifies the context of prop_1 but does not alter the LTL structure.
